coding_notes: Replicate 3 verdicts. Factual-quantitative stable across replicates; small numerical wobble within published ranges. C2.6 hom-OR called variably by vendor: r1 deepseek=2.94, r3 deepseek=2.80; r1 anthropic=5.97, r3 anthropic=3.26. Treating these as substantive agreement (specific value named, within 2x of each other) by normalizing alternative to 'hom-OR-named'.
ground_truth_notes: Standard reference values for ABCG2 / Q141K.

claims.claim_text: ABCG2 mediates approximately one-third (30-33%) of total uric acid excretion.

openai.value_quoted: ~30% with caveat that this is inferential (Ichida 2012)

anthropic.value_quoted: ~10% mouse / uncertain in humans; ~2/3 extra-renal phenotype - lenient affirm because uncertainty explicit

claims.claim_text: Q141K reduces transporter activity by ~50% relative to wild-type.
Q141K minor allele frequency in East Asian populations: ~0.29-0.32.
Published OR for gout/hyperuricemia associated with Q141K is in the ~1.6-2.3 range (varying by population/method).
Homozygote (KK) gout OR is substantially higher than heterozygote (QK). Vendors that name a specific value should agree within 2x.

deepseek.value_quoted: Hom AA OR=2.80 (Woodward NZ-Caucasian) - vs r1 OR=2.94, similar but distinct

gemini.value_quoted: Per-allele only, no clean hom split

openai.value_quoted: Mild dysfunction (heterozygote) OR~2.35; mentions hom higher but no clean hom number

anthropic.value_quoted: Hom OR=3.26 (Matsuo Ann Rheum Dis 2010) - vs r1 OR=5.97

claims.claim_text: Gut/intestine is the dominant site of ABCG2-mediated uric acid excretion (as opposed to kidney).
